key_insight: H-Res adapts frozen Transformer memories by steering activations on the manifold, shaping the effective energy landscape without changing the model’s weights or sequence length.
engineering_relevance: This offers a low-overhead adaptation strategy for large pretrained models, relevant to deploying stable, task-specific AI systems in domains where preserving core model behavior matters.
method: Learn a state-dependent vector field over token activations.
Steer trajectories toward task-specific attractor basins in the residual/activation manifold.
Preserve global equilibrium and attention entropy while enabling adaptation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 26% better performance than global weight modification on associative retrieval tasks and claims better scalability to structured domains.
watch_item: The approach assumes the learned steering field generalizes across tasks and inputs, so implementation stability and robustness outside the reported retrieval settings should be validated.
paper_title: Parallel Manifold Steering: Efficient Adaptation of Large Associative Memories via Residual Energy Shaping
